rulings.Case Title: SCOTT DAVID ROBISON VS. CITGO PETROLEUM CORPORATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 7, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANT BENJAMIN MOORE & CO.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD THE CLAIMS WITH MORE PARTICULARITY IN COMPLIANCE WITH BOCKRATH. rulings.Case Title: IN RE: WEDBUSH SECURITIES INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Heairng On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award; Declaration Of John W. Stenson (Volumes 1 Through 4)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 7, 2011, line 15, PETITIONER WEDBUSH SECURITIES INC. Notice Of Heairng On Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award. PETITIONER'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE ARBITRATION PANEL DID NOT ABUSE THEIR DISCRETION OR EXCEED THEIR POWERS BY DENYING PETITIONER'S REQUEST FOR POSTPONEMENT. PETITIONERS FAILED TO SHOW GOOD CAUSE TO THE PANEL WHICH WOULD WARRANT A POSTPONEMENT. THE PANEL ALSO PROPERLY EXCLUDED PETITIONER'S EVIDENCE AS TO THE INVESTIGATION INTO RESPONDENTS TRADING ACTIVITIES. PETITIONER FAILED TO DISCLOSE THIS EVIDENCE TO RESPONDENT 20 DAYS PRIOR TO THE FIRST DAY OF ARBITRATION. ACCORDINGLY, THE ARBITRATION PANEL WAS REQUIRED TO EXCLUDE THE EVIDENCE UNDER FINRA RULE 13514 IN ABSENCE OF ANY SHOWING BY PETITIONER THAT THERE WAS GOOD CAUSE FOR THE PANEL TO ADMIT THE EVIDENCE IN LIGHT OF THE RULE VIOLATION. FINALLY, THERE WAS NO BASIS FOR DISQUALIFICATION OF ARBITRATOR HITCHCOCK. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: WEDBUSH SECURITIES INC.
